goal: Breakthrough QFL privacy research informed by LASA-QTERM: improve SHARD Stage-2 under partial/realistic observations, or novel privacy-preserving mechanisms (defenses, leak budgets, detection) with measurable superiority over baselines—not documentation-only limits.

breakthrough_signals.signal_1: ABT-1 assignment barrier + LASA-QTERM T1 impossibility cross-cite
breakthrough_signals.signal_2: Oracle GARD-SPARSE 75% row reduction @ MSE 0.10 (conditional honest-incidence QFL)
breakthrough_signals.signal_3_secondary: LEAK-CERT T1p empirical coverage (honest 2x criterion C fails)

acceptance.breakthrough_definition: At least one of: (A) >=25% reduction in observed rows vs SHARD Stage-2 at equal snapshot MSE; (B) defense increases attacker snapshot MSE by >=50% vs undefended (attack worse) at <=10% normalized utility loss; (C) novel privacy certificate with proved bound tighter than naive mean-only baseline by >=2x

domain_notes: Build on qfl-terminal-snapshot impossibility (T1), T1p/T1b tiers, parent TALON. Use vendor/shard_sim via code/_paths.py. Prior GARD/JASPER in parent rounds/05-06.
